data.title: [Bug creation]: Agent ignores ticket title format warning -> non-standard bug titles committed to tracker
data.description:
## Incident Overview
When .claude/scripts/dso ticket create bug emits a warning that the title does not match the recommended [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result] pattern, agents proceed without correcting the title. The warning is printed to stdout but produces no behavioral change.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Agent calls: .claude/scripts/dso ticket create bug 'some free-form title'
2. CLI prints: 'Warning: Bug title does not match recommended pattern: [Component]: [Condition] -> [Observed Result]'
3. Agent reads the ticket ID from the output and moves on
4. Ticket is created with non-standard title

## Expected
Agent corrects the title (via ticket edit) before proceeding, or formats the title correctly on the first call.

## Actual
Warning is ignored. Non-standard title persists in the tracker.

## Proposed Fix Approach
Use dso:bot-psychologist to identify why agents skip the correction step — likely the warning appears after the ticket ID on the same output, and the agent pattern-matches the ID and stops reading. Fix may involve: (a) making the CLI exit non-zero on format violations so the agent sees a failure, (b) moving the warning above the ticket ID, or (c) strengthening the bug-creation guidance in CLAUDE.md or the create-bug skill.
